oai_dc:dc.dc:title: The Resilient Saint Vincent’s Amazon
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Waugh, David
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: When St. Vincent and the Grenadines gained independence in 1979 there was no doubt which avian species to name as the national bird: the striking St. Vincent Amazon. This article reviews the species’ conservation history, the role of the St. Vincent Forestry Department and supporting organizations, and the parrot’s apparent resilience after volcanic eruption impacts. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): AFA Watchbird April 2025
